title: How To Use CGMissingDataR
text: CGMissingDataR supports two related continuous glucose monitoring missing-data workflows: benchmarking imputation methods masking known glucose values, imputing glucose values already missing user data. current workflow functions accept raw timestamp column create TimeSeries TimeDifferenceMinutes internally. can choose model outputs return models. default models = "mice_only" quick MICE-run. Use models = "" run MICE-, Random Forest, kNN, XGBoost, LightGBM, ARIMA, pass subset models = c("mice_only", "rf").

title: How To Use CGMissingDataR
text: installation, make sure modeling dependencies available: Install development version CGMissingDataR GitHub: Load package:

title: How To Use CGMissingDataR
text: package includes two small multi-subject CGM example datasets. contain raw timestamps, precomputed TimeSeries TimeDifferenceMinutes columns. generated time features created inside imputation functions. CGMExampleData intended benchmarking. glucose values complete, benchmark function can mask known values compare imputed values truth. CGMExampleData2 columns includes deterministic missing glucose values. intended demonstrating imputation actual missing target values.
code: data("CGMExampleData")  data.frame(   Dataset = "CGMExampleData",   Rows = nrow(CGMExampleData),   Subjects = length(unique(CGMExampleData$USUBJID)),   MissingGlucose = sum(is.na(CGMExampleData$LBORRES)),   MissingPercent = round(mean(is.na(CGMExampleData$LBORRES)) * 100, 1) ) #>          Dataset Rows Subjects MissingGlucose MissingPercent #> 1 CGMExampleData  500        5              0              0  head(CGMExampleData) #>   USUBJID LBORRES             Time AGE hba1c #> 1      11     150 2020:01:16:00:00  34   6.4 #> 2      11     134 2020:01:16:00:05  34   6.4 #> 3      11     125 2020:01:16:00:10  34   6.4 #> 4      11     132 2020:01:16:00:15  34   6.4 #> 5      11     132 2020:01:16:00:20  34   6.4 #> 6      11     132 2020:01:16:00:25  34   6.4 data("CGMExampleData2")  data.frame(   Dataset = "CGMExampleData2",   Rows = nrow(CGMExampleData2),   Subjects = length(unique(CGMExampleData2$USUBJID)),   MissingGlucose = sum(is.na(CGMExampleData2$LBORRES)),   MissingPercent = round(mean(is.na(CGMExampleData2$LBORRES)) * 100, 1) ) #>           Dataset Rows Subjects MissingGlucose MissingPercent #> 1 CGMExampleData2  500        5             50             10  head(CGMExampleData2) #>   USUBJID LBORRES             Time AGE hba1c #> 1      11     150 2020:01:16:00:00  34   6.4 #> 2      11     134 2020:01:16:00:05  34   6.4 #> 3      11     125 2020:01:16:00:10  34   6.4 #> 4      11     132 2020:01:16:00:15  34   6.4 #> 5      11     132 2020:01:16:00:20  34   6.4 #> 6      11     132 2020:01:16:00:25  34   6.4

title: How To Use CGMissingDataR
text: Use run_comprehensive_imputation_benchmark() glucose values known want mask portion compare model performance. example masks 5% glucose rows using random masking runs available methods. Smaller model iteration counts used example stays reasonably quick. results table contains one row per selected model mask rate. selected model also gets completed data frame benchmark_out$imputed_data. model-specific data frames include original observed glucose value, whether row masked, model-specific completed value.
code: benchmark_out <- run_comprehensive_imputation_benchmark(   CGMExampleData,   target_col = "LBORRES",   feature_cols = c("AGE", "hba1c"),   id_col = "USUBJID",   time_col = "Time",   time_format = "yyyy:mm:dd:hh:nn",   mask_rates = 0.05,   mask_type = "random",   models = "all",   rf_n_estimators = 25,   xgb_nrounds = 25,   lgb_nrounds = 25 ) benchmark_out$results #>   MaskRate MaskType                                    Method      MAPE #> 1       5%   random     ARIMA(4,1,0) on MICE-completed target 1.9770378 #> 2       5%   random      MICE + KNN (engineered lag features) 0.5088444 #> 3       5%   random MICE + LightGBM (engineered lag features) 0.8230684 #> 4       5%   random       MICE + RF (engineered lag features) 0.3515021 #> 5       5%   random  MICE + XGBoost (engineered lag features) 0.8499923 #> 6       5%   random MICE-only (base features; impute LBORRES) 2.0344169 #>          R2         MRD MaskedCount #> 1 0.9496530 0.019770378          25 #> 2 0.9965445 0.005088444          25 #> 3 0.9946395 0.008230684          25 #> 4 0.9976854 0.003515021          25 #> 5 0.9945244 0.008499923          25 #> 6 0.9606598 0.020344169          25 names(benchmark_out$imputed_data) #> [1] "mice_only" "rf"        "knn"       "xgboost"   "lightgbm"  "arima" head(   benchmark_out$imputed_data$rf[     ,     c(       "USUBJID", "Time", ".Masked", "ObservedValue", "ImputedValue",       "TimeSeries", "TimeDifferenceMinutes"     )   ] ) #>   USUBJID             Time .Masked ObservedValue ImputedValue TimeSeries #> 1      11 2020:01:16:00:00   FALSE           150          150      31075 #> 2      11 2020:01:16:00:05   FALSE           134          134      31080 #> 3      11 2020:01:16:00:10   FALSE           125          125      31085 #> 4      11 2020:01:16:00:15   FALSE           132          132      31090 #> 5      11 2020:01:16:00:20   FALSE           132          132      31095 #> 6      11 2020:01:16:00:25   FALSE           132          132      31100 #>   TimeDifferenceMinutes #> 1                     0 #> 2                     5 #> 3                     5 #> 4                     5 #> 5                     5 #> 6                     5

title: How To Use CGMissingDataR
text: Use run_missing_glucose_imputation() data already contains missing glucose values. function returns imputed values calculate accuracy metrics, true values missing rows unknown. example uses CGMExampleData2, 50 500 glucose rows missing. summary table reports missingness seen preprocessing. returned imputed_data object named list one completed data frame per selected method. real-imputation output, original target column left unchanged. means LBORRES still NA glucose originally missing, ImputedValue contains completed glucose value.
code: impute_out <- run_missing_glucose_imputation(   CGMExampleData2,   target_col = "LBORRES",   feature_cols = c("AGE", "hba1c"),   id_col = "USUBJID",   time_col = "Time",   time_format = "yyyy:mm:dd:hh:nn",   models = "all",   rf_n_estimators = 25,   xgb_nrounds = 25,   lgb_nrounds = 25 ) impute_out$summary #>                                      Method MissingRate MissingCount RowsUsed #> 1 MICE-only (base features; impute LBORRES)         0.1           50      500 #> 2       MICE + RF (engineered lag features)         0.1           50      500 #> 3      MICE + KNN (engineered lag features)         0.1           50      500 #> 4  MICE + XGBoost (engineered lag features)         0.1           50      500 #> 5 MICE + LightGBM (engineered lag features)         0.1           50      500 #> 6     ARIMA(4,1,0) on MICE-completed target         0.1           50      500 names(impute_out$imputed_data) #> [1] "mice_only" "rf"        "knn"       "xgboost"   "lightgbm"  "arima" rf_missing_rows <- impute_out$imputed_data$rf[   impute_out$imputed_data$rf$.Missing,   c(     "USUBJID", "Time", ".Missing", "LBORRES", "ObservedValue",     "ImputedValue", "TimeSeries", "TimeDifferenceMinutes"   ) ]  head(rf_missing_rows) #>    USUBJID             Time .Missing LBORRES ObservedValue ImputedValue #> 10      11 2020:01:16:00:45     TRUE      NA            NA       135.08 #> 31      11 2020:01:16:02:30     TRUE      NA            NA        82.52 #> 32      11 2020:01:16:02:35     TRUE      NA            NA        75.36 #> 33      11 2020:01:16:02:40     TRUE      NA            NA       155.76 #> 34      11 2020:01:16:02:45     TRUE      NA            NA       123.36 #> 55      11 2020:01:16:04:30     TRUE      NA            NA        78.68 #>    TimeSeries TimeDifferenceMinutes #> 10      31120                     5 #> 31      31225                     5 #> 32      31230                     5 #> 33      31235                     5 #> 34      31240                     5 #> 55      31345                     5

text: Saraswat S, Hasin Shahed Shad, Zhang X (2026). CGMissingDataR: Benchmark Impute Missing Glucose Values CGM Data. R package version 0.0.1.9000, https://zhanglabuky.github.io/CGMissingDataR/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {CGMissingDataR: Benchmark and Impute Missing Glucose Values in CGM Data},   author = {Shubh Saraswat and {Hasin Shahed Shad} and Xiaohua Douglas Zhang},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.1.9000},   url = {https://zhanglabuky.github.io/CGMissingDataR/}, }

title: Welcome to CGMissingDataR
text: CGMissingDataR supports continuous glucose monitoring-style missing-data workflows. can benchmark imputation methods masking known glucose values, impute glucose values already missing user data. Current workflows include: run_comprehensive_imputation_benchmark() artificial masking benchmarks, run_missing_glucose_imputation() real missing glucose values, model selection models = "mice_only", subset models, models = "", automatic creation TimeSeries TimeDifferenceMinutes raw timestamp column.

title: Welcome to CGMissingDataR
text: installation, make sure modeling dependencies available: Install development version CGMissingDataR GitHub: Install CGMissingDataR CRAN :

title: Welcome to CGMissingDataR
text: vignette main tutorial includes runnable examples, dataset summaries, benchmark results, real-imputation output: https://zhanglabuky.github.io/CGMissingDataR/articles/--Use-CGMissingDataR.html

title: Welcome to CGMissingDataR
text: changelog available : https://zhanglabuky.github.io/CGMissingDataR/news/index.html

title: Example dataset for CGMissingData — CGMExampleData
text: small multi-subject CGM dataset intended benchmark examples tests.

title: Example dataset for CGMissingData — CGMExampleData
text: data frame 500 rows 5 variables: USUBJID Numeric subject identifier. LBORRES Laboratory Observed Result Glucose (numeric). Time Raw timestamp yyyy:mm:dd:hh:nn format. AGE Synthetic age years. hba1c Synthetic HbA1c value.

what: Example dataset with missing glucose values — CGMExampleData2
title: Example dataset with missing glucose values — CGMExampleData2
text: small multi-subject CGM dataset intended real missing-value imputation examples. structure CGMExampleData, LBORRES contains deterministic missing glucose values.

title: Example dataset with missing glucose values — CGMExampleData2
Example dataset with missing glucose values — CGMExampleData2
text: data frame 500 rows 5 variables: USUBJID Numeric subject identifier. LBORRES Laboratory Observed Result Glucose (numeric), deterministic missing values. Time Raw timestamp yyyy:mm:dd:hh:nn format. AGE Synthetic age years. hba1c Synthetic HbA1c value.

title: Example dataset with missing glucose values — CGMExampleData2

what: CGMissingDataR: Benchmark and Impute Missing Glucose Values in CGM Data — CGMissingDataR-package
title: CGMissingDataR: Benchmark and Impute Missing Glucose Values in CGM Data — CGMissingDataR-package
text: Benchmarks imputes missing glucose values repeated-measures continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) data. package can mask known glucose values using random, contiguous block, gap-distribution block missingness compare imputation performance, can impute glucose values already missing user data. Workflows create time-series features raw timestamps, support model selection, return model-specific completed data sets. Methods include multiple imputation chained equations (MICE; Azur et al. (2011) doi:10.1002/mpr.329 ), Random Forest regression (Breiman (2001) doi:10.1023/:1010933404324 ), k-nearest-neighbor regression (Zhang (2016) doi:10.21037/atm.2016.03.37 ), XGBoost (Chen Guestrin (2016) doi:10.1145/2939672.2939785 ), LightGBM (Ke et al. (2017) doi:10.5555/3294996.3295074 ), ARIMA forecasting forecast framework (Hyndman Khandakar (2008) doi:10.18637/jss.v027.i03 ).

title: CGMissingDataR: Benchmark and Impute Missing Glucose Values in CGM Data — CGMissingDataR-package
text: Maintainer: Shubh Saraswat shubh.saraswat00@gmail.com (ORCID) [copyright holder] Authors: Hasin Shahed Shad hasin.shad@uky.edu Xiaohua Douglas Zhang douglas.zhang@uky.edu (ORCID)

title: Run comprehensive imputation benchmark — run_comprehensive_imputation_benchmark
text: Benchmarks target imputation random, contiguous block, gap-distribution block masking. workflow imputes masked target MICE, recomputes lag features completed target series, evaluates MICE-, Random Forest, kNN, XGBoost, LightGBM, ARIMA predictions full target series.

title: Run comprehensive imputation benchmark — run_comprehensive_imputation_benchmark
code: run_comprehensive_imputation_benchmark(   data,   target_col,   feature_cols = NULL,   id_col = "USUBJID",   time_col = "Time",   time_format = "yyyy:mm:dd:hh:nn",   time_unit = "minute",   mask_rates = c(0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.4),   mask_type = c("random", "block", "gap_block"),   models = "mice_only",   rf_n_estimators = 200,   knn_k = 7,   xgb_nrounds = 300,   lgb_nrounds = 400,   arima_order = c(4L, 1L, 0L),   seed = 42,   lag_k = c(1, 2, 3),   add_rollmean = TRUE,   roll_window = 3,   gap_bins = list(c(1, 5), c(6, 35), c(36, NA)),   gap_probs = c(0.5923, 0.2569, 0.1509),   open_cap = 0.5 )

title: Run comprehensive imputation benchmark — run_comprehensive_imputation_benchmark
text: data data.frame, object coercible data.frame, path CSV file. target_col Single character string: target column mask impute. feature_cols Character vector base feature columns excluding target_col. NULL, columns except target_col, time_col, generated time features used. id_col Character string: subject identifier column used lag features. time_col Character string: raw timestamp column convert TimeSeries. time_format Character string passed CGManalyzer::timeSeqConversion.fn(). time_unit Character string passed CGManalyzer::timeSeqConversion.fn(). Use "minute" "second". mask_rates Numeric vector (0, 1): target-row masking rates. mask_type One "random", "block", "gap_block". models Character vector models return. Use "mice_only", "rf", "knn", "xgboost", "lightgbm", "arima", "". MICE always run internally models depend MICE-completed target. rf_n_estimators Integer: number random forest trees. used models includes "rf" "". knn_k Integer: number kNN neighbors. used models includes "knn" "". xgb_nrounds Integer: number XGBoost boosting rounds. used models includes "xgboost" "". lgb_nrounds Integer: number LightGBM boosting rounds. used models includes "lightgbm" "". arima_order Integer vector length 3 forecast::Arima(). used models includes "arima" "". seed Integer seed masking, MICE, model reproducibility. lag_k Integer vector target lags compute. add_rollmean Logical: add rolling mean prior target values. roll_window Integer rolling mean window. gap_bins List length-2 vectors defining gap-block size bins. gap_probs Numeric probabilities gap_bins. open_cap Numeric cap used open-ended gap bin.

title: Run comprehensive imputation benchmark — run_comprehensive_imputation_benchmark
text: list containing results, data.frame columns MaskRate, MaskType, Method, MAPE, R2, MRD, MaskedCount; imputed_data, named list model-specific completed data.frames.

title: Run comprehensive imputation benchmark — run_comprehensive_imputation_benchmark
text: returned imputed_data object named list one data.frame per selected model. data.frame stacks rows across mask rates. unmasked rows, ImputedValue equals ObservedValue; masked rows, ImputedValue method-specific imputed predicted target value. MRD follows comprehensive script convention: sum(abs(true - pred) / abs(true)) / length(true), zero true values excluded numerator retained denominator. MAPE MRD * 100. R2 computed full-length prediction vector.

title: Run comprehensive imputation benchmark — run_comprehensive_imputation_benchmark
code: data("CGMExampleData") out <- run_comprehensive_imputation_benchmark(   CGMExampleData,   target_col = "LBORRES",   feature_cols = c("AGE", "hba1c"),   id_col = "USUBJID",   time_col = "Time",   time_format = "yyyy:mm:dd:hh:nn",   mask_rates = 0.05,   models = "all",   rf_n_estimators = 25,   xgb_nrounds = 25,   lgb_nrounds = 25 ) out$results #>   MaskRate MaskType                                    Method      MAPE #> 1       5%   random     ARIMA(4,1,0) on MICE-completed target 1.9770378 #> 2       5%   random      MICE + KNN (engineered lag features) 0.5088444 #> 3       5%   random MICE + LightGBM (engineered lag features) 0.8230684 #> 4       5%   random       MICE + RF (engineered lag features) 0.3515021 #> 5       5%   random  MICE + XGBoost (engineered lag features) 0.8499923 #> 6       5%   random MICE-only (base features; impute LBORRES) 2.0344169 #>          R2         MRD MaskedCount #> 1 0.9496530 0.019770378          25 #> 2 0.9965445 0.005088444          25 #> 3 0.9946395 0.008230684          25 #> 4 0.9976854 0.003515021          25 #> 5 0.9945244 0.008499923          25 #> 6 0.9606598 0.020344169          25 names(out$imputed_data) #> [1] "mice_only" "rf"        "knn"       "xgboost"   "lightgbm"  "arima"     head(out$imputed_data$rf) #>   MaskRate MaskType                              Method .Masked USUBJID LBORRES #> 1       5%   random MICE + RF (engineered lag features)   FALSE      11     150 #> 2       5%   random MICE + RF (engineered lag features)   FALSE      11     134 #> 3       5%   random MICE + RF (engineered lag features)   FALSE      11     125 #> 4       5%   random MICE + RF (engineered lag features)   FALSE      11     132 #> 5       5%   random MICE + RF (engineered lag features)   FALSE      11     132 #> 6       5%   random MICE + RF (engineered lag features)   FALSE      11     132 #>               Time AGE hba1c TimeSeries TimeDifferenceMinutes LBORRES_lag1 #> 1 2020:01:16:00:00  34   6.4      31075                     0           NA #> 2 2020:01:16:00:05  34   6.4      31080                     5          150 #> 3 2020:01:16:00:10  34   6.4      31085                     5          134 #> 4 2020:01:16:00:15  34   6.4      31090                     5          125 #> 5 2020:01:16:00:20  34   6.4      31095                     5          132 #> 6 2020:01:16:00:25  34   6.4      31100                     5          132 #>   LBORRES_lag2 LBORRES_lag3 LBORRES_rollmean_3 ObservedValue ImputedValue #> 1           NA           NA                 NA           150          150 #> 2           NA           NA                 NA           134          134 #> 3          150           NA                 NA           125          125 #> 4          134          150           136.3333           132          132 #> 5          125          134           130.3333           132          132 #> 6          132          125           129.6667           132          132

what: Impute real missing glucose values — run_missing_glucose_imputation
title: Impute real missing glucose values — run_missing_glucose_imputation
text: Imputes existing missing values target glucose column using time-feature, lag-feature, model workflow run_comprehensive_imputation_benchmark(). function calculate accuracy metrics true values originally missing glucose rows unknown.

title: Impute real missing glucose values — run_missing_glucose_imputation
code: run_missing_glucose_imputation(   data,   target_col,   feature_cols = NULL,   id_col = "USUBJID",   time_col = "Time",   time_format = "yyyy:mm:dd:hh:nn",   time_unit = "minute",   models = "mice_only",   rf_n_estimators = 200,   knn_k = 7,   xgb_nrounds = 300,   lgb_nrounds = 400,   arima_order = c(4L, 1L, 0L),   seed = 42,   lag_k = c(1, 2, 3),   add_rollmean = TRUE,   roll_window = 3 )

title: Impute real missing glucose values — run_missing_glucose_imputation
text: data data.frame, object coercible data.frame, path CSV file. target_col Single character string: target column missing values impute. feature_cols Character vector base feature columns excluding target_col. NULL, columns except target_col, time_col, generated time features used. id_col Character string: subject identifier column used time gaps lag features. time_col Character string: raw timestamp column convert TimeSeries. time_format Character string passed CGManalyzer::timeSeqConversion.fn(). time_unit Character string passed CGManalyzer::timeSeqConversion.fn(). Use "minute" "second". models Character vector models return. Use "mice_only", "rf", "knn", "xgboost", "lightgbm", "arima", "". MICE always run internally models depend MICE-completed target. rf_n_estimators Integer: number random forest trees. used models includes "rf" "". knn_k Integer: number kNN neighbors. used models includes "knn" "". xgb_nrounds Integer: number XGBoost boosting rounds. used models includes "xgboost" "". lgb_nrounds Integer: number LightGBM boosting rounds. used models includes "lightgbm" "". arima_order Integer vector length 3 forecast::Arima(). used models includes "arima" "". seed Integer seed MICE model reproducibility. lag_k Integer vector target lags compute. add_rollmean Logical: add rolling mean prior target values. roll_window Integer rolling mean window.

title: Impute real missing glucose values — run_missing_glucose_imputation
text: list containing summary, data.frame columns Method, MissingRate, MissingCount, RowsUsed; imputed_data, named list model-specific completed data.frames.

title: Impute real missing glucose values — run_missing_glucose_imputation
text: returned imputed_data object named list one data.frame per selected model. original target column kept unchanged. ObservedValue contains original target values, including NA glucose missing, ImputedValue contains completed model-specific target values.

title: Impute real missing glucose values — run_missing_glucose_imputation
code: data("CGMExampleData2") out <- run_missing_glucose_imputation(   CGMExampleData2,   target_col = "LBORRES",   feature_cols = c("AGE", "hba1c"),   id_col = "USUBJID",   time_col = "Time",   time_format = "yyyy:mm:dd:hh:nn",   models = c("mice_only", "rf"),   rf_n_estimators = 25 ) out$summary #>                                      Method MissingRate MissingCount RowsUsed #> 1 MICE-only (base features; impute LBORRES)         0.1           50      500 #> 2       MICE + RF (engineered lag features)         0.1           50      500 names(out$imputed_data) #> [1] "mice_only" "rf"        head(out$imputed_data$rf) #>                                Method .Missing USUBJID LBORRES             Time #> 1 MICE + RF (engineered lag features)    FALSE      11     150 2020:01:16:00:00 #> 2 MICE + RF (engineered lag features)    FALSE      11     134 2020:01:16:00:05 #> 3 MICE + RF (engineered lag features)    FALSE      11     125 2020:01:16:00:10 #> 4 MICE + RF (engineered lag features)    FALSE      11     132 2020:01:16:00:15 #> 5 MICE + RF (engineered lag features)    FALSE      11     132 2020:01:16:00:20 #> 6 MICE + RF (engineered lag features)    FALSE      11     132 2020:01:16:00:25 #>   AGE hba1c TimeSeries TimeDifferenceMinutes LBORRES_lag1 LBORRES_lag2 #> 1  34   6.4      31075                     0           NA           NA #> 2  34   6.4      31080                     5          150           NA #> 3  34   6.4      31085                     5          134          150 #> 4  34   6.4      31090                     5          125          134 #> 5  34   6.4      31095                     5          132          125 #> 6  34   6.4      31100                     5          132          132 #>   LBORRES_lag3 LBORRES_rollmean_3 ObservedValue ImputedValue #> 1           NA                 NA           150          150 #> 2           NA                 NA           134          134 #> 3           NA                 NA           125          125 #> 4          150           136.3333           132          132 #> 5          134           130.3333           132          132 #> 6          125           129.6667           132          132

what: Run missingness benchmark (target-masking with LAG features)  — run_missingness_benchmark
title: Run missingness benchmark (target-masking with LAG features)  — run_missingness_benchmark
text: function deprecated. Use run_comprehensive_imputation_benchmark() benchmark comparisons run_missing_glucose_imputation() real missing glucose values. function implements missingness benchmarking masking target column various rates evaluating imputation predictive performance MICE, Random Forest, KNN methods. Additionally, includes LAG features target variable assess impact imputation prediction. function returns data.frame summarizing Mask Rate, Method, MRD (Mean Relative Difference), Masked Count method mask rate.

title: Run missingness benchmark (target-masking with LAG features)  — run_missingness_benchmark
code: run_missingness_benchmark(   data,   target_col,   feature_cols = NULL,   id_col = "USUBJID",   time_col = "TimeSeries",   mask_rates = c(0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.4),   mask_type = c("random", "block"),   rf_n_estimators = 400,   knn_k = 7,   seed = 42,   lag_k = c(1, 2, 3),   add_rollmean = TRUE,   roll_window = 3 )

title: Run missingness benchmark (target-masking with LAG features)  — run_missingness_benchmark
text: data data.frame (object coercible data.frame), path CSV file. target_col Single character string: name outcome column mask/impute (e.g., "LBORRES", "Glucose"). feature_cols Character vector base feature columns (excluding target). NULL, uses columns except target_col. id_col Character string: subject identifier column used LAG features (default "USUBJID"). time_col Character string: time-ordering column used LAG features (default "TimeSeries"). mask_rates Numeric vector (0, 1): fraction rows mask (default 0.05, 0.10, 0.20, 0.30, 0.40). mask_type One "random" "block". rf_n_estimators Integer: number trees random forest (default 400). knn_k Integer: number neighbors kNN (default 7). seed Integer: random seed used MICE models (default 42). lag_k Integer vector lags compute target (default c(1,2,3)). add_rollmean Logical: add rolling mean feature prior target values (default TRUE). roll_window Integer: rolling window length rollmean (default 3).

title: Run missingness benchmark (target-masking with LAG features)  — run_missingness_benchmark
text: data.frame columns: MaskRate, Method, MRD, MaskedCount.

title: Run missingness benchmark (target-masking with LAG features)  — run_missingness_benchmark
text: LAG features computed using data.table::shift() (fast lag/lead). rolling mean computed data.table::frollmean() using align="right" fill=NA.

title: CGMissingDataR (development version)
text: Added run_comprehensive_imputation_benchmark() benchmarking target glucose imputation random, single-block, gap-distribution block masking. Added run_missing_glucose_imputation() imputing glucose values already missing user data. Added automatic raw timestamp conversion CGManalyzer::timeSeqConversion.fn() create TimeSeries, plus within-subject TimeDifferenceMinutes. Added model selection models argument. default models = "mice_only"; use models = "" run MICE-, Random Forest, kNN, XGBoost, LightGBM, ARIMA. Updated benchmark real-imputation outputs return nested model-specific imputed_data data frames. Added CGMExampleData2, 500-row multi-subject CGM example dataset deterministic missing glucose values real-imputation examples. Deprecated run_missingness_benchmark(). remains available backward compatibility now warns users prefer run_comprehensive_imputation_benchmark() benchmark comparisons run_missing_glucose_imputation() real missing glucose values. Preparing version 0.0.2 CGMissingDataR transferring GitHub Repository fixing URLs.

title: CGMissingDataR 0.0.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-02-03 Initial package creation preparing CRAN submission.
